title: Value Network Construction: Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions

sections.title: CFJ Statement
sections.content:
> Acquire, preserve, and present cultural, historical, natural, and scientific heritage to the public through curated exhibitions, conservation, and educational programming.

**L7 Ecosystem name:** Museum & Heritage Ecosystem
**L7 description:** Economic community of museums, historic sites, conservators, lenders, donors, regulators, and visitors coordinating the long-term safeguarding and public presentation of irreplaceable cultural and scientific heritage.

---

sections.title: Step A.1 — L6 Core Process Steps
sections.content:
Sequential operational chain from object intake to public presentation.

| Node | Name | Job family | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6a | Collection Acquisition & Registration | Receive, document, and formally accession incoming objects into the collection | Universal — all institution types accession objects or site records |
| L6b | Conservation & Collections Care | Stabilize, treat, and store collection objects to prevent deterioration and ensure long-term integrity | Universal — mandatory for all collection types |
| L6c | Exhibition Development & Installation | Design, plan, fabricate, and install gallery or site presentations for visitor access | Universal — delivering the public-facing output |
| L6d | Visitor Experience & Public Programming | Deliver admissions, guided tours, education programs, and public events | Universal — generates revenue and public mandate |
| L6e | Collection Access & Research Services | Manage scholarly access, object loans to partner institutions, and provenance research requests | Variable — scale depends on institution type and collection significance |

---

sections.title: Step A.2 — L6 Horizontal Process Steps
sections.content:
Parallel functions that must run continuously across all core steps.

| Node | Name | Job family | Why horizontal |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6H-SEC | Building Security & Life Safety | Protect collections, staff, and visitors against theft, unauthorized access, fire, and emergency exit misuse at all times | Fire code + heritage protection mandates 24/7 coverage — never a sequential step |
| L6H-ENV | Environmental & Climate Control | Maintain conservation-grade temperature, humidity, and light levels continuously across collection and storage areas | Collections degrade irreversibly without stable environment — runs parallel to all core steps |
| L6H-FM | Facility Management & Maintenance | Maintain building fabric, utilities, and physical infrastructure in operational and compliant condition | Heritage building constraints and listed status create continuous compliance obligations |
| L6H-IT | Digital & Collections IT | Provide Collections Management System, visitor-facing digital, and network infrastructure for all operational systems | All core steps depend on CMS or digital connectivity |

> Removing L6H-SEC makes the institution legally non-compliant (fire code, insurance, heritage legislation); removing L6H-ENV causes irreversible artifact damage. Both are horizontal — they run across every core step, never replacing any.

---

sections.title: Step A.3 — L5 Equipment per L6 Node
sections.content:
### L6a — Collection Acquisition & Registration

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Collections Management System (CMS) | The Museum System / TMS (Gallery Systems) | 30% | LOW | Analyst estimate; dominant in large art museums |
| | PastPerfect (Altus Systems) | 25% | | Strong in history/natural history |
| | MuseumPlus / Qi (Zetcom) | 15% | | European institutions |
| | KE EMu (Axiell) | 15% | | Natural history / sciences |
| | Other / custom | 15% | | |
| Object Photography & Documentation Station | Camera + copy stand + controlled lighting rig | 70% | LOW | Industry standard for 2D/3D documentation |
| | 3D structured-light scanner (for objects) | 20% | | Growing for sculptures, artifacts |
| | Photogrammetry rig | 10% | | Archaeological / open-air sites |
| Condition Reporting Tool | Tablet + CMS-integrated app (Axiell, Mimsy) | 55% | LOW | Replacing paper forms in most institutions |
| | Paper condition report form | 35% | | Legacy; still common in smaller museums |
| | Standalone app (MicroMusée, MuseReport) | 10% | | |

### L6b — Conservation & Collections Care

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Environmental Monitoring System | HOBO data loggers (Onset) | 35% | LOW | [Onset smart monitoring for museums](https://www.onsetcomp.com/blog/modern-monitoring-solutions-for-museum-and-preservation-management) |
| | Hanwell / ElvaX wireless loggers | 20% | | |
| | Conserv (cloud-native) | 15% | | Emerging; strong in US art museums |
| | Eltek Darca / other wired | 15% | | Legacy installations |
| | Other | 15% | | |
| Archival Storage Racking | Compact mobile shelving (mechanical compactus) | 50% | LOW | Space-efficient; museum standard |
| | Static metal shelving | 35% | | Low-cost option |
| | Archival flat files / map cabinets | 15% | | For oversized works on paper |
| Integrated Pest Management Equipment | Sticky traps + insect monitoring stations | 60% | LOW | Primary detection layer |
| | Anoxic treatment chambers | 25% | | Active eradication; heritage collections |
| | Freeze treatment unit | 15% | | For textile / organic materials |

### L6c — Exhibition Development & Installation

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Exhibition Design Software | AutoCAD / Revit (Autodesk) | 45% | LOW | Default in large institutions with design teams |
| | SketchUp (Trimble) | 25% | | Smaller teams; rapid concept |
| | Vectorworks (Nemetschek) | 15% | | Theatre/events crossover; exhibitions |
| | Other / hand drafting | 15% | | |
| Audio-Visual Presentation System | AV projection (Christie, Barco) | 35% | LOW | Immersive galleries; large-format |
| | Touchscreen interactive kiosk | 30% | | Interpretive / educational content |
| | LED video panel | 25% | | Permanent installations |
| | 360° immersive / spatial audio | 10% | | Emerging; flagship exhibitions |
| Gallery Lighting | LED tunable-white luminaires (Erco, Zumtobel) | 65% | LOW | Conservation-grade (no UV/IR) |
| | Fibre-optic spotlight (legacy) | 20% | | Sensitive textiles, manuscripts |
| | Incandescent / halogen (legacy) | 15% | | Declining; retrofit replacement ongoing |

### L6d — Visitor Experience & Public Programming

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Ticketing & Admissions System | Altru (Blackbaud) | 20% | LOW | Nonprofit museums |
| | Tessitura Network | 15% | | Performing arts / large institutions |
| | SIRIUSWARE | 10% | | National parks, science centers |
| | Other (Spektrix, TicketSolve, Shopify) | 55% | | Fragmented; many local/bespoke |
| Audio Guide System | App-based (Bloomberg Connects, izi.TRAVEL) | 45% | LOW | [Bloomberg Connects dominates US art museums](https://www.asisonline.org/security-management-magazine/latest-news/today-in-security/2024/september/improving-museum-security/) |
| | Dedicated handheld audio guide device | 40% | | Common in history and natural history |
| | Printed guide only | 15% | | Low-budget institutions |
| Visitor Counting & Flow Analytics | Thermal / infrared people counter | 45% | LOW | Primary entrance counting |
| | Wi-Fi / BLE anonymized tracking | 30% | | Gallery-level dwell analytics |
| | Manual clicker count | 25% | | Smaller / historic sites |

### L6e — Collection Access & Research Services

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Loan & Object Movement Tracking | CMS-integrated loan module (TMS, PastPerfect) | 70% | LOW | Majority use CMS module |
| | Standalone loan software (Argus Loans) | 20% | | |
| | Manual / spreadsheet | 10% | | |
| RFID / Barcode Object Tagging | Barcode label + handheld scanner | 50% | LOW | Cost-effective; most institutions |
| | RFID tag + fixed / handheld reader | 30% | | Growing for high-value collections |
| | Manual checklist only | 20% | | Smaller historic sites |

### L6H-SEC — Building Security & Life Safety ← Türwächter anchor

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Emergency Exit Security Devices** | Basic mechanical door alarm (no monitoring) | 45% | LOW | Common in heritage buildings with wiring constraints |
| | **Alarmed exit hardware with IoT monitoring** ← **Türwächter** | 20% | | Battery-powered wireless; ideal for listed buildings |
| | Wired panic bar / crash bar alarm | 25% | | New-build or major refurbishment |
| | No dedicated exit monitoring | 10% | | Small historic house museums |
| Intruder Detection & Alarm System | PIR / microwave motion detectors | 55% | MEDIUM | [Museum security systems overview](https://www.omnilert.com/blog/museum-security-systems) |
| | Vibration / seismic sensors (for cases) | 25% | | Jewelry, small valuables |
| | Glass-break detectors | 20% | | Historic buildings with large windows |
| Electronic Access Control | IP-based (ASSA ABLOY, Dormakaba, Salto) | 55% | MEDIUM | [Advanced museum security 2025](https://aaaguards.com/blogs/advanced-security-for-art-galleries-museums/) |
| | Legacy magnetic card reader | 30% | | Older installations |
| | Mobile credential (ASSA ABLOY Seos) | 15% | | Growing for staff-only zones |
| Video Surveillance (CCTV) | IP camera / NVR (Axis, Bosch, Hikvision) | 65% | MEDIUM | [NPS Museum Security 2024](https://www.nps.gov/subjects/museums/upload/MHI_Ch14_Museum_Security.pdf) |
| | Analog / DVR (legacy) | 25% | | Aging institutions upgrading 1990s systems |
| | Cloud-managed camera (Verkada) | 10% | | Emerging |
| Fire Detection & Alarm System | Addressable panel (Honeywell, Siemens, Hochiki) | 60% | MEDIUM | [Museum fire safety reference](https://www.wonderfulmuseums.com/museum/fire-at-museum/) |
| | Conventional zone panel | 30% | | Smaller historic sites |
| | Wireless fire system | 10% | | Heritage retrofit where wiring is prohibited |

> Heritage buildings (listed / protected) disproportionately use wireless and battery-powered solutions (wireless fire, wireless exit alarms) because routing wired conduit through historic fabric requires planning permission and heritage authority sign-off. The Türwächter's surface-mount, battery-powered profile aligns exactly with these constraints. Source: [WBDG Historic Preservation Life Safety](https://wbdg.org/design-objectives/historic-preservation/accommodate-life-safety-security-needs), [GSA Fire Safety Retrofitting](https://www.gsa.gov/real-estate/historic-preservation/historic-preservation-policy-tools/preservation-tools-resources/technical-procedures/fire-safety-retrofitting-guidelines-for-historic-buildings)

### L6H-ENV — Environmental & Climate Control

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| HVAC Precision Climate System | Chiller plant + dedicated AHU (precision RH ±5%) | 45% | LOW | Major art / natural history museums |
| | VRF/split with supplemental humidification | 30% | | Mid-size museums; heritage buildings |
| | Conventional commercial HVAC (no precision RH) | 25% | | Small institutions; lower-value collections |
| Humidifier / Dehumidifier | Integrated HVAC humidification section | 40% | LOW | Purpose-built galleries |
| | Standalone ultrasonic / steam humidifiers | 35% | | Retrofit; seasonal correction |
| | Silica gel passive buffering | 25% | | Historic house museums; low-budget |
| UV & Light Control | UV-filtering window film / glazing | 40% | LOW | [Heritage fire & light protection](https://www.buildingconservation.com/articles/fire/fire_protection.htm) |
| | Motorized blackout / solar-control blinds | 35% | | |
| | Specialized gallery lighting controls (daylight sensor + dimming) | 25% | | |

### L6H-FM — Facility Management & Maintenance

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Building Management System (BMS) | Honeywell Building Technologies | 30% | LOW | Dominant across institutional buildings |
| | Siemens Desigo CC | 25% | | |
| | Johnson Controls Metasys | 25% | | |
| | Other / no BMS | 20% | | Smaller historic sites |
| CMMS (Preventive Maintenance) | Archibus | 20% | LOW | Higher-education / large institutions |
| | IBM Maximo | 15% | | |
| | Hippo CMMS / other cloud | 15% | | |
| | Manual / spreadsheet | 35% | | Common in smaller museums |
| | Other | 15% | | |

### L6H-IT — Digital & Collections IT

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Network Infrastructure (LAN/WiFi) | Cisco Meraki | 35% | LOW | Common in institutional campus settings |
| | Aruba / HPE | 30% | | |
| | Ruckus (CommScope) | 20% | | |
| | Other | 15% | | |
| Visitor-Facing Digital Platform | Institution-managed website + app (bespoke) | 40% | LOW | Larger museums with dev capability |
| | Bloomberg Connects (free platform) | 20% | | Rapidly growing in US/EU art museums |
| | Google Arts & Culture partnership | 20% | | Secondary channel; not primary |
| | Other (Cuseum, Smartify) | 20% | | |

---

sections.title: Step A.4 — Output Type Activation Matrix
sections.content:
Output types: **ART** = Art museum | **NAT** = Natural history / science museum | **HIST** = History museum | **ARCH** = Archaeological site / open-air museum | **SCI** = Science & technology center

| L6 Node | ART | NAT | HIST | ARCH | SCI | Notes |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|---|
| L6a Acquisition & Registration | x | x | x | x | x | Universal — all collect or record |
| L6b Conservation & Collections Care | x | x | x | x | x | Universal — all have objects or sites to protect |
| L6c Exhibition Development & Installation | x | x | x | x | x | Universal — public exhibition is the core output |
| L6d Visitor Experience & Programming | x | x | x | x | x | Universal |
| L6e Collection Access & Research | x | x | x | x | — | Science centers often have no permanent loan collection |
| L6H-SEC Building Security & Life Safety | x | x | x | x | x | Universal — mandatory by fire code and heritage law |
| L6H-ENV Environmental & Climate Control | x | x | x | — | x | Open-air archaeological sites have no enclosed climate zone |
| L6H-FM Facility Management | x | x | x | x | x | Universal |
| L6H-IT Digital & Collections IT | x | x | x | x | x | Universal |

**Insight:** L6H-ENV is the only partially non-universal step — outdoor archaeological sites lack enclosed climate zones. L6H-SEC activates universally: every institution type is subject to fire code and emergency exit regulations regardless of collection type or building status. The Türwächter addresses a universally mandatory requirement.

---

sections.title: Step 04a — L4 Fill
sections.content:
Every L5 below has ≥3 L4 children (structural floor = 2, target = 3).

### Collections Management System (CMS) → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Object Catalogue Module | Core accession records: title, date, provenance, materials, images |
| Inventory & Location Module | Storage location, movement history, object condition status |
| Loan & Exhibition Module | Outgoing/incoming loan workflow, packing/condition reports |
| Reporting Engine | Accession statistics, collection value summaries, audit outputs |
| Integration API | Data exchange with image libraries, partner institutions, public portals |

### Environmental Monitoring System → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Temperature Sensor | Ambient temperature data logger (±0.1°C resolution) |
| Relative Humidity Probe | Capacitive RH sensor with dew-point derivation |
| Light Level / UV Meter | Lux and UV index monitoring (conservation threshold alerting) |
| Data Logger Controller | Central aggregation hub; local alarm relay |
| Remote Monitoring Software | Dashboard with threshold alerts, trend visualisation, export |

### Emergency Exit Security Devices → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Mechanical Guard Housing | Spring-loaded handle-locking mechanism (power-independent; EN 179/EN 1125 compliant) |
| Alarm Horn | Piezoelectric acoustic alert transducer (~95 dB; activates on 45° guard swing) |
| IoT Radio Transmitter | 868 MHz wireless state broadcast module; transmits guard state and alarm events |
| Battery Pack | 9V portable power supply for alarm + IoT module |
| Monitoring Software Interface | Dashboard / BMS integration for remote status aggregation across all exit points |

### Intruder Detection & Alarm System → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| PIR / Dual-Tech Motion Detector | Passive infrared or combined MW/PIR motion sensor |
| Alarm Control Panel | Zone controller, event log, staff/police communication relay |
| Audible Sounder / Strobe | Internal/external alarm device on intrusion confirmation |
| Case Vibration Sensor | Seismic / shock sensor for glass cases and display cabinets |
| Central Monitoring Interface | Staff/guard monitoring station with map-based zone display |

### Electronic Access Control → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Card / Credential Reader | RFID or proximity reader at controlled doors |
| Access Controller / Door Panel | Zone-level authorization engine and relay driver |
| Electric Lock / Maglock | Electromagnetic or electromechanical release hardware |
| Access Management Software | Staff database, permission levels, audit log |
| Key Override Cylinder | Mechanical fallback for power failure (heritage requirement) |

### Video Surveillance (CCTV) → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| IP Camera (dome / PTZ) | ≥4MP resolution; wide dynamic range for mixed gallery lighting |
| NVR / Video Management Software | Video storage, retrieval, multi-site management |
| Video Analytics Engine | Motion detection, dwell-time analytics, object-removal detection |
| Display Console | Guard station monitor array for real-time observation |
| Secure Storage Enclosure | Tamper-resistant NVR housing (data chain of custody for incidents) |

### Fire Detection & Alarm System → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Addressable Control Panel | Zone controller + power supply + event log (EN 54 compliant) |
| Smoke Detector Head | Optical scatter detector (sensitive settings for irreplaceable collections) |
| Heat Detector Head | Rate-of-rise / fixed-temperature element |
| Alarm Sounder (Horn / Bell) | Audible alert on zone activation |
| Manual Call Point | Break-glass or push-button activation |

### HVAC Precision Climate System → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Chiller Unit | Refrigerant-based cooling plant serving conservation-grade AHU |
| Air Handling Unit (AHU) | Air distribution with HEPA/activated-carbon filtration |
| Humidifier / Dehumidifier Module | Moisture addition/removal within ±5% RH set point |
| Zone Damper Controller | Variable air volume / zone-level set-point management |
| Building Automation Controller | Set-point management, alarm interface, remote override |

### Building Management System (BMS) → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| HVAC Zone Controller | Equipment-level PLC for AHU, chiller, humidifier per zone |
| Energy Metering Module | kWh monitoring per zone (sustainability reporting, budget control) |
| Alarm Management Interface | Fault / alert aggregation for facilities team |
| Remote Access Client | Mobile/web dashboard for off-site building operations |

---

sections.title: Step 04b — Completeness Check
sections.content:
| Domain | Status |
|---|---|
| Core process chain (L6a–L6e) | Complete — 5 sequential steps from intake to research access |
| Horizontal safety (L6H-SEC) | Complete — fire, intruder, exit, access control, CCTV |
| Environmental preservation (L6H-ENV) | Complete — climate, humidity, UV/light |
| Facility management (L6H-FM) | Complete — BMS, CMMS |
| Digital infrastructure (L6H-IT) | Complete — CMS, LAN, visitor platform |
| L4 fill | Complete — all key L5 nodes have ≥3 L4 children |

---

sections.title: QA Self-Check
sections.content:
| # | Check | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 | CFJ is industry-level | PASS — no technology reference; describes entire NAICS 712110 purpose |
| Q2 | No orphan nodes | PASS — all units have valid parent references |
| Q3 | Valid level transitions (L7→L6, L6→L5, L5→L4) | PASS — no level skipping |
| Q4 | L4 children per L5 ≥2 | PASS — all key L5 nodes have ≥3 L4 children |
| Q5 | No solution-specific L7 or L6 names | PASS — "Museum & Heritage Ecosystem" is industry-level, not technology-specific |
| Q6 | Three-question naming test | PASS — "Collections Management System", "Conservation", "Admissions System", "PIR Motion Detector" pass all three questions |
| Q7 | Unit count in range (15–60) | PASS — estimated ~45 units across L7–L4 |

---

sections.title: Türwächter Entry Point Summary
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **VN position** | L5 within L6H-SEC (Building Security & Life Safety) |
| **L5 node** | Emergency Exit Security Devices |
| **L5 alternative category** | Alarmed exit hardware with IoT monitoring |
| **Estimated alternative share** | ~20% (battery-powered wireless; growing in heritage buildings) |
| **Key differentiator in this market** | Surface-mounted, battery-powered, no structural modification required — uniquely suited to heritage-listed buildings where wiring through historic fabric requires planning permission |
| **Primary constraint in this market** | C5 (Surface Mount acceptable — no flush integration required; heritage buildings actually prefer non-invasive) + C8 (GDPR for EU public buildings) |
| **Competing alternatives displaced** | Basic mechanical door alarm (no remote monitoring) — 45% share, widely used but provides no centralized visibility |

---

rows.Name: Collection Acquisition & Registration
rows.Job family: Receive, document, and formally accession incoming objects into the collection
rows.Notes: Universal — all institution types accession objects or site records

rows.Name: Conservation & Collections Care
rows.Job family: Stabilize, treat, and store collection objects to prevent deterioration and ensure long-term integrity
rows.Notes: Universal — mandatory for all collection types

rows.Name: Exhibition Development & Installation
rows.Job family: Design, plan, fabricate, and install gallery or site presentations for visitor access

rows.Name: Visitor Experience & Public Programming
rows.Job family: Deliver admissions, guided tours, education programs, and public events
rows.Notes: Universal — generates revenue and public mandate

rows.Name: Collection Access & Research Services
rows.Job family: Manage scholarly access, object loans to partner institutions, and provenance research requests
rows.Notes: Variable — scale depends on institution type and collection significance

rows.Name: Building Security & Life Safety
rows.Job family: Protect collections, staff, and visitors against theft, unauthorized access, fire, and emergency exit misuse at all times
rows.Why horizontal: Fire code + heritage protection mandates 24/7 coverage — never a sequential step

rows.Name: Environmental & Climate Control
rows.Job family: Maintain conservation-grade temperature, humidity, and light levels continuously across collection and storage areas
rows.Why horizontal: Collections degrade irreversibly without stable environment — runs parallel to all core steps

rows.Name: Facility Management & Maintenance
rows.Job family: Maintain building fabric, utilities, and physical infrastructure in operational and compliant condition
rows.Why horizontal: Heritage building constraints and listed status create continuous compliance obligations

rows.Name: Digital & Collections IT
rows.Job family: Provide Collections Management System, visitor-facing digital, and network infrastructure for all operational systems
rows.Why horizontal: All core steps depend on CMS or digital connectivity

rows.Source: Analyst estimate; dominant in large art museums

rows.Alternatives: Camera + copy stand + controlled lighting rig

rows.Source: Replacing paper forms in most institutions

rows.Share: Legacy; still common in smaller museums
Emerging; strong in US art museums

rows.Source: Default in large institutions with design teams

rows.Share: Common in history and natural history

rows.Source: Common in heritage buildings with wiring constraints

rows.Share: Battery-powered wireless; ideal for listed buildings

rows.L5: Wired panic bar / crash bar alarm

rows.Share: Heritage retrofit where wiring is prohibited

rows.L5: Specialized gallery lighting controls (daylight sensor + dimming)

rows.Share: Rapidly growing in US/EU art museums

rows.Notes: Universal — all have objects or sites to protect
Universal — public exhibition is the core output
Science centers often have no permanent loan collection
Universal — mandatory by fire code and heritage law
Open-air archaeological sites have no enclosed climate zone

rows.Status: Complete — 5 sequential steps from intake to research access
Complete — fire, intruder, exit, access control, CCTV
Complete — all key L5 nodes have ≥3 L4 children
PASS — no technology reference; describes entire NAICS 712110 purpose
PASS — all units have valid parent references

rows.Check: No solution-specific L7 or L6 names
rows.Status: PASS — "Museum & Heritage Ecosystem" is industry-level, not technology-specific
PASS — "Collections Management System", "Conservation", "Admissions System", "PIR Motion Detector" pass all three questions

rows.Value: L5 within L6H-SEC (Building Security & Life Safety)
Alarmed exit hardware with IoT monitoring
Surface-mounted, battery-powered, no structural modification required — uniquely suited to heritage-listed buildings where wiring through historic fabric requires planning permission
C5 (Surface Mount acceptable — no flush integration required; heritage buildings actually prefer non-invasive) + C8 (GDPR for EU public buildings)
Basic mechanical door alarm (no remote monitoring) — 45% share, widely used but provides no centralized visibility

entities.market_name: Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions
entities.naics_title: Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions
entities.cfj_statement: Acquire, preserve, and present cultural, historical, natural, and scientific heritage to the public through curated exhibitions, conservation, and educational programming.

units.name: Museum & Heritage Ecosystem
units.description: Economic community of museums, historic sites, conservators, lenders, donors, regulators, and visitors coordinating the long-term safeguarding and public presentation of irreplaceable cultural and scientific heritage.

units.name: Collection Acquisition & Registration
units.description: Receive, document, and formally accession incoming objects into the collection.

units.name: Conservation & Collections Care
units.description: Stabilize, treat, and store collection objects to prevent deterioration and ensure long-term integrity.

units.name: Exhibition Development & Installation
units.description: Design, plan, fabricate, and install gallery or site presentations for visitor access.

units.name: Collections Management System
units.description: Central digital repository for all collection data: accession records, location, condition, loans, and provenance.

units.name: Object Photography & Documentation Station
units.description: Controlled-environment imaging setup for object documentation at intake.

units.name: Condition Reporting Tool

units.name: Environmental Monitoring System
units.description: Distributed sensor network measuring temperature, RH, light, and UV across storage and gallery zones.

units.name: Archival Storage Racking
units.description: Shelving and racking systems for organized, accessible, and protective object storage.

units.name: Integrated Pest Management Equipment
units.description: Trapping, monitoring, and treatment equipment to prevent biological damage to organic collection materials.

units.name: Exhibition Design Software
units.description: CAD/BIM tools for planning gallery layouts, circulation routes, and installation specifications.

units.name: Audio-Visual Presentation System
units.description: Projection, LED, kiosk, or immersive AV equipment for content delivery in gallery spaces.

units.name: Gallery Lighting
units.description: Conservation-grade luminaires with UV/IR filtering and tunable colour temperature.

units.name: Ticketing & Admissions System

units.name: Audio Guide System
units.description: App-based or dedicated device delivering interpretation and narrative to visitors in galleries.

units.name: Visitor Counting & Flow Analytics

units.name: Loan & Object Movement Tracking

units.name: NVR / Video Management Software
units.description: Video storage, retrieval, and multi-site or multi-building management platform.

units.name: Video Analytics Engine
units.description: Motion detection, dwell-time analytics, and object-removal detection for collections protection.

units.name: Display Console
units.description: Guard station monitor array for real-time observation and incident response.

units.name: Addressable Control Panel
units.description: Zone controller, power supply, and event log (EN 54 compliant).

units.name: Smoke Detector Head
units.description: Optical scatter detector (sensitive settings for irreplaceable collections).

units.name: Heat Detector Head
units.description: Rate-of-rise or fixed-temperature element for kitchens, plant rooms.

units.name: Alarm Sounder
units.description: Audible alert on zone activation; visual strobe for accessibility compliance.

units.name: Manual Call Point
units.description: Break-glass or push-button activation by occupant at emergency points.

units.name: Chiller Unit
units.description: Refrigerant-based cooling plant serving conservation-grade AHU.

units.name: Air Handling Unit
units.description: Air distribution with HEPA/activated-carbon filtration for collection-grade air quality.

units.name: Humidifier / Dehumidifier Module
units.description: Moisture addition/removal to maintain ±5% RH set point.

units.name: Zone Damper Controller
units.description: Variable air volume / zone-level set-point management.

units.name: Building Automation Controller
units.description: Set-point management, alarm interface, and remote override for conservation zones.

units.name: HVAC Zone Controller
units.description: Equipment-level PLC for AHU, chiller, and humidifier per zone.

units.name: Energy Metering Module
units.description: kWh monitoring per zone for sustainability reporting and budget control.

units.name: Alarm Management Interface
units.description: Fault and alert aggregation dashboard for facilities team.

units.name: Remote Access Client
units.description: Mobile/web dashboard for off-site building operations and emergency response.

units.name: Core Switch
units.description: Layer-3 distribution switch (server room / IDF) connecting all building systems.

units.name: Wireless Access Point
units.description: 802.11ax (Wi-Fi 6) AP for staff and visitor connectivity.

units.name: Firewall / Security Gateway
units.description: Perimeter security protecting CMS and collections data from external threats.
